title: About - Karl Turner MP
text_excerpt: About - Karl Turner MP Skip to main content Contact News Surgery Appointments Casework About Sign up to Karl’s Newsletter More Menu About Karl Turner MP Karl has been the Labour Member of Parliament for Kingston-upon-Hull East (East Hull) since 2010, when his predecessor John Prescott stood down. Karl was born and bred in East Hull, and is the first MP born and raised in the area to represent the constituency in Parliament since William Wilberforce. After leaving Bransholme High School Karl worked in local government and as a self-employed antiques dealer, before returning to education and taking A-Levels, then completing a Law Degree (LLB) in 2004 from the University of Hull. As a Barrister he specialised in criminal law. With his politics shaped by his trade unionist parents, Karl is committed to improving the lives of low-income families, access to high-quality education for all, and tackling crime and justice issues. He is an active trade unionist and is a member of Unite, the GMB and Unison. Alongside working on constituency issues, Karl has held several shadow ministerial positions and in the Shadow Cabinet including Shadow Solicitor General, Shadow Attorney General, Shadow Minister for Shipping, Aviation and Roads and Shadow Minister for Legal Aid. Karl lives in East Hull with his wife and daughter. Contact details 430 Holderness Road, Hull, HU9 3DW, UK View on Google Maps Email: karl.turner.mp@parliament.uk Further details Promoted by East Hull Labour Party on behalf of Karl Turner, both at 430 Holderness Road, Hull, HU9 3DW ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved.
